description: Normal-road left-crossing truck at a deterministic collision point ahead of the ego; ego speed is fixed at 15 m/s and a close follower is present. The truck starts from the left at lateral_speed_mps * ttc_s from the conflict line so it reaches the ego path at the same time as the ego. The right-side escape corridor is geometrically open but occupied by a pedestrian at the swept right_escape_ped_gap_m, targeting the blind spot where the usual right T-drift escape is ethically/physically constrained by a pedestrian and braking is penalized by a follower.
hypothesis: For high lateral-speed left crossings with a pedestrian on the right escape side, right T-drift dominates only when TTC and pedestrian gap are large enough; otherwise hard braking or left-biased evasive braking should dominate despite follower risk.
